evals.name: yoruba-corpus-build
evals.prompt: Help me build a monolingual training corpus for Yoruba. What sources should I use and how should I dedup?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend MADLAD-400 + CulturaX + Wikipedia + MasakhaNER (mention overlap warning between MADLAD/CulturaX); (2) flag Bible-NLP availability + register-drift risk if > 30%; (3) require ethics routing per source; (4) recommend GlotLID at paragraph granularity; (5) MinHash threshold 0.9 (NOT 0.8) for low-resource; (6) confusable fold via linguistic-scripts BEFORE MinHash; (7) document-level LID is insufficient for code-switched Yoruba/English content.

evals.name: contamination-flores-on-llama3
evals.prompt: We're evaluating Llama-3 (cutoff March 2024) on FLORES-200. Are we okay?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) flag side-(b) contamination risk: FLORES released 2022-07, well before Llama-3 cutoff; (2) note FLORES is in many 2024+ pretrain mixes (The Pile, MADLAD, etc.); (3) recommend NTREX-128 or custom held-out instead; (4) explain 13-gram check is necessary even if alternative not used (transparent reporting); (5) note: still report FLORES for comparability but as a *lower bound* on quality.

evals.name: cebuano-wikipedia-trap
evals.prompt: Cebuano Wikipedia has 6 million articles. Should I treat Cebuano as a Class 5 high-resource language for tokenizer training?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — flag bot-generated nature of Cebuano Wikipedia (Lsjbot); (2) explain raw size ≠ corpus quality; (3) recommend downweighting or excluding bot-generated entries; (4) note Joshi class needs benchmark + tooling signals, not just Wiki size; (5) recommend register-balance audit; (6) similar warning applies to Waray, Volapük.
